Amazon: Title must not contain promotional phrases like 'Free Shipping', 'Best Seller', or 'Hot Item'.
Title length must not exceed 200 characters.
Bullet points (Features) should be sentence-cased and informative.
Do not use HTML tags in the Title.
Main image requirements (white background) should be noted in strategy.
Shopify: Title should be SEO-friendly, ideally under 70 characters to prevent truncation in search results.
Description should use H2 tags for section headers to improve readability.
Focus on brand voice and storytelling rather than just specs.
Etsy: Title should include descriptive keywords at the beginning (Front-loading).
Focus on 'handmade', 'vintage', 'custom', or 'gift' aspects if applicable.
Tone should be personal, human, and community-focused.
Instagram Shop: Captions should be engaging and concise.
Include 5-30 relevant hashtags at the very bottom of the post.
Do not include non-clickable URLs in the caption body.
Focus on visual aesthetics and lifestyle benefits.
Google Shopping: Title must be accurate and describe the physical product (Brand + Gender + Product Type + Color + Size).
No promotional text ('Buy now', 'Sale') in the title.
Avoid block capitals and gimmicky punctuation.
Walmart Marketplace: Title should be 50-75 characters for best optimization.
Description should be professional and include keywords naturally.
Email Newsletter: Subject line (Title) must be catchy, short, and urgency-driven if needed.
Body text should be personal, direct, and have a clear Call to Action (CTA).
Landing Page: Focus heavily on conversion, benefits, and social proof.
Clear separation between headlines and body copy.
Default: Maintain a consistent tone suitable for general e-commerce.
